reasoning: Bulgaria's Energy Minister explicitly stated in May 2025 that the Botas contract cannot be terminated without paying 4 billion leva upfront, while simultaneously acknowledging Bulgargaz cannot make payments and faces bankruptcy. BOTAS has agreed to offer amendment options as of March 2025. With only 25 days until July 1, formal cancellation appears unlikely given the high termination cost and ongoing renegotiation discussions. However, the extreme financial pressure (€130M debt, bankruptcy risk) and previous BOTAS rejection of renegotiation in August 2024 create material risk of de facto cancellation through breach or arbitration. Probability at 55% reflects slightly higher likelihood of some form of announcement than not, given the crisis-level situation.
